lenses.guidance: The seven-day ordination (v 35) and twice-daily offering (vv 38-42) frame priesthood with calendar. The narrative structure binds time itself to atonement; Israel measures days by sacrifice, not just sundial.

Blood is applied to ear, thumb, and toe (v 20) — hearing, doing, walking all consecrated. The pattern of priestly purification covers the whole life. Christ's blood (Heb 10:14) does what these repeated rites only enacted.

v 36's 'sin offering for atonement' begins seven days of purification before priesthood begins. Christ's once-for-all sacrifice (Heb 7:27) abolishes the cycle while fulfilling its meaning — purified priesthood is now his.
The lamb morning and evening (vv 38-42) trains daily rhythm — every dawn and dusk shaped by sacrifice. Today, anchor prayer to the same rhythm; let morning and evening name themselves with the cross.
